req.title: System-Admin updates org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a UI form update flow for org-announcements by a system-admin. I derived tests using: EP for the happy-path (valid admin submits all mandatory fields); NEG for unauthenticated access (401) and forbidden role (403); BVA for the title field at its maximum allowed length boundary; DT for missing mandatory fields triggering 400; and STC to verify the audit record state transition on success. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that set up and tear down state.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_org_announcement_success
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements
Open the edit form for the existing announcement
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
expected: HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains entity id
Announcement is updated in the UI list
Audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-050: authenticated admin can update announcement
R-WEB-050: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-050: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
def test_admin_updates_org_announcement_success(admin_page, existing_announcement, audit_log_client):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    <open_edit_form>(page, ann_id)
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", "Updated Title EP")
    page.fill("[data-testid='body']", "Updated body text EP")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain entity id"
    page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='success-toast']")
    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=body["id"], action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_org_announcement
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no session cookie / token)
An existing org-announcement id is known
actions: Send PUT request to /org-announcements/{id} without authentication header
Inspect HTTP response status
expected: HTTP response status is 401
Response body contains error indicating unauthenticated access
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_org_announcement(api_client, existing_announcement):
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    payload = {"title": "Hack attempt", "body": "Should fail"}
    resp = api_client.put(
        f"/org-announcements/{ann_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={},
        skip_auth=True
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    err = resp.json()
    assert "error" in err or "message" in err, "Response should contain error detail"

tests.name: test_title_at_maximum_length_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Maximum title length is defined as 255 characters
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements
Open the edit form for the existing announcement
Set title to exactly 255 characters
Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
expected: HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains entity id
No validation error is shown
trace: R-WEB-050: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-050: response 200 or 201 on valid input at boundary
tests.executable:
def test_title_at_maximum_length_boundary_accepted(admin_page, existing_announcement):
    MAX_LEN = 255
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    boundary_title = "B" * MAX_LEN
    assert len(boundary_title) == MAX_LEN
    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    <open_edit_form>(page, ann_id)
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", boundary_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='body']", "Valid body text for BVA test")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {resp.status}"
    assert "id" in resp.json()
    assert page.query_selector("[data-testid='field-error-title']") is None

tests.name: test_title_exceeds_maximum_length_rejected
preconditions: Maximum title length is defined as 255 characters
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements
Open the edit form for the existing announcement
Set title to 256 characters (one over boundary)
Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
expected: HTTP response status is 400
Validation error message is displayed for the title field
trace: R-WEB-050: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-050: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
def test_title_exceeds_maximum_length_rejected(admin_page, existing_announcement):
    MAX_LEN = 255
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    over_boundary_title = "X" * (MAX_LEN + 1)
    assert len(over_boundary_title) == MAX_LEN + 1
    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    <open_edit_form>(page, ann_id)
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", over_boundary_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='body']", "Valid body text")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 400, f"Expected 400 over max boundary, got {resp.status}"
    assert page.query_selector("[data-testid='field-error-title']") is not None

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_title_field_returns_400
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements
Open the edit form for the existing announcement
Clear the mandatory title field
Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
expected: HTTP response status is 400
Validation error message is displayed for the title field
Announcement is NOT updated
trace: R-WEB-050: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-050: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
def test_missing_mandatory_title_field_returns_400(admin_page, existing_announcement):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    original_title = existing_announcement["title"]
    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    <open_edit_form>(page, ann_id)
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", "")
    page.fill("[data-testid='body']", "Valid body DT test")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 400, f"Expected 400 for missing title, got {resp.status}"
    assert page.query_selector("[data-testid='field-error-title']") is not None
    refreshed = <fetch_announcement>(ann_id)
    assert refreshed["title"] == original_title, "Announcement must not be updated on validation failure"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_org_announcement
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: viewer (non-admin)
An existing org-announcement id is known
actions: Send PUT request to /org-announcements/{id} with viewer-role credentials
Inspect HTTP response status
expected: HTTP response status is 403
Response body contains error indicating forbidden access
trace: R-WEB-050: error pathway 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_audit_record_state_transition_on_update
preconditions: No prior UPDATE audit entry exists for the announcement id
actions: Record current audit log count for the announcement id
Navigate to /org-announcements and open the edit form
Fill all mandatory fields with new valid data
Submit the form and confirm success response
Query audit log for a new UPDATE entry for the entity id
expected: Audit log has exactly one new UPDATE entry after the operation
Audit entry actor is the authenticated admin user
Audit entry entity_id matches the announcement id
State transitions: PENDING_UPDATE -> UPDATED reflected in audit action field
trace: R-WEB-050: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
def test_audit_record_state_transition_on_update(admin_page, existing_announcement, audit_log_client, admin_user):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = existing_announcement["id"]
    before_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE")
    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    <open_edit_form>(page, ann_id)
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", "STC Audit Title")
    page.fill("[data-testid='body']", "STC Audit Body")
    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "POST")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    assert resp_info.value.status in (200, 201)
    after_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert after_count == before_count + 1, "Exactly one new audit entry must be created"
    entry = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert entry["actor_id"] == admin_user["id"]
    assert entry["entity_id"] == str(ann_id)
    assert entry["action"] == "UPDATE"
